USA.name: Donald Trump
USA.title: President of the United States
USA.tagline: Calls Iran's military 'gone' — navy, air force, air defense, communications destroyed. House backed his war authority.
USA.domestic_detail: House defeated the Massie-Khanna resolution to curb his authority, with several Democrats joining Republicans. Public support holding but US casualties could shift opinion.
USA.global_detail: NATO activated ballistic missile defense. UK offering bases. But international concern growing over civilian casualties, especially the girls' school attack.
USA.bio: On Day 7, Trump declared US forces are 'totally demolishing' Iran 'far ahead of schedule.' He detailed: 24 ships sunk, no air force, no air defense, no communications, 60% of missiles and 64% of launchers gone. The House voted to let Epic Fury continue. He called on the IRGC to 'lay down their arms — they're only going to be killed.' He told reporters the war could run longer than 4-5 weeks if needed.

USA.name: Pete Hegseth
USA.title: Secretary of Defense
USA.tagline: Visited CENTCOM in Tampa — declared munitions 'full up' and US 'will is ironclad.'
USA.domestic_detail: Confirmed amid controversy. His CENTCOM visit and bullish public statements reinforce the administration's escalatory posture.
USA.global_detail: European allies wary. Israel views him as the most aligned US Defense Secretary in decades.
USA.bio: Hegseth visited CENTCOM headquarters at MacDill Air Force Base in Tampa on March 6, meeting with Admiral Cooper. He declared: 'We have no shortage of munitions. Our stockpiles of defensive and offensive weapons allow us to sustain this campaign as long as we need to. Our will is ironclad. We are built for this fight and we are in it to win it.'

USA.name: Marco Rubio
USA.title: Secretary of State
USA.tagline: Managing diplomacy across 12 countries now attacked by Iran while the US dismantles Iranian military capability.
USA.domestic_detail: Bipartisan respect as foreign policy voice. Seen as the diplomatic counterbalance to Hegseth's hawkishness.
USA.global_detail: Gulf allies appreciate engagement. European counterparts strained by the scale of operations.
USA.bio: Rubio is managing the diplomatic complexity of a war that now involves 12 countries attacked by Iran. He coordinates the GCC relationship, NATO's ballistic missile defense activation, and the UK's offer of bases for defensive strikes.

ISR.name: Benjamin Netanyahu
ISR.title: Prime Minister of Israel
ISR.tagline: Approved ground invasion of South Lebanon while Israeli air forces achieve 'almost complete air superiority' over Iran.
ISR.domestic_detail: Two-front war may temporarily boost support. Corruption trials continue. Evacuation of 800,000+ in Lebanon is a massive humanitarian decision.
ISR.global_detail: Western allies increasingly concerned about the scale of Lebanon operations and civilian displacement.
ISR.bio: Netanyahu approved Defense Minister Katz's instruction for the IDF to occupy additional positions inside Lebanon. Three IDF divisions are positioned for a ground invasion south of the Litani River. The IDF Chief of Staff reported destroying 80% of Iran's air defenses and achieving 'almost complete air superiority.' Netanyahu said: 'We have additional surprising moves which I do not intend to reveal. We will pursue all our enemies and reach them.'

ISR.name: Israel Katz
ISR.title: Minister of Defense
ISR.tagline: Instructed IDF to occupy additional positions in Lebanon — ground invasion preparations underway with three divisions.
ISR.domestic_detail: His instruction to expand the Lebanese ground operation, approved by Netanyahu, is the most significant escalation since October 2024.
ISR.global_detail: The 800,000-person evacuation order has drawn sharp international criticism and humanitarian concern.
ISR.bio: Katz instructed the IDF, with Netanyahu's approval, to occupy additional positions inside Lebanese territory. The 91st, 210th, and 146th Divisions are deployed for what analysts assess will be a ground invasion south of the Litani River — the largest Israeli ground operation in Lebanon since 2006.

IRN.name: Ali Khamenei
IRN.title: Supreme Leader (KILLED Feb 28, 2026)
IRN.tagline: Ruled Iran for 36 years. Killed alongside his wife in U.S.-Israeli airstrike. Over 48 senior officials also killed.
IRN.domestic_detail: Revered by hardliners. Deeply unpopular among reformists and youth. His death created a massive power vacuum.
IRN.global_detail: Viewed by the West as architect of Iran's nuclear ambitions, proxy wars, and domestic repression.
IRN.bio: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28, 2026 when U.S.-Israeli strikes destroyed his Tehran compound. His wife, daughter, grandchild, and other family members were also killed. At least 48 senior officials died in the campaign.

IRN.name: Masoud Pezeshkian
IRN.title: President of Iran (Leadership Council Chair)
IRN.tagline: Leading Iran's crisis response as the country's military is systematically dismantled — 1,230+ dead, navy destroyed.
IRN.domestic_detail: A reformist forced into wartime leadership. Constrained by IRGC power but responsible for maintaining government function under bombardment.
IRN.global_detail: Western diplomats had seen him as a potential partner. The war has eliminated that possibility for now.
IRN.bio: Pezeshkian chairs the temporary leadership council managing Iran's response as the country's conventional military is systematically destroyed — navy gone, air force gone, 80% of air defenses degraded. The ICRC released audio of a Tehran civilian describing 'constant explosions.' A girls' school attack killed 168. Pezeshkian faces the impossible task of maintaining governance while Iran's military capability collapses.

IRN.name: Ayatollah Alireza Arafi
IRN.title: Leadership Council Member (Potential Supreme Leader Successor)
IRN.tagline: Deputy chairman of the Assembly of Experts and Guardian Council member — the frontrunner for next Supreme Leader.
IRN.domestic_detail: Well-connected in the clerical establishment. Serves on both the Guardian Council and the Assembly of Experts.
IRN.global_detail: Western intelligence still assessing. His dual role on Guardian Council and Assembly of Experts makes him the most powerful figure in the succession process.
IRN.bio: Arafi serves on both the Guardian Council (which vets laws and election candidates) and as deputy chairman of the Assembly of Experts (which selects the Supreme Leader). This dual position makes him uniquely powerful in the succession process. He was appointed to the temporary leadership council to represent the clerical establishment.

SAU.name: Mohammed bin Salman
SAU.title: Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia
SAU.tagline: Invoking UN Article 51 self-defense as Iranian strikes hit energy infrastructure and Gulf solidarity hardens.
SAU.domestic_detail: Iranian attacks on Saudi soil are strengthening nationalist support. Vision 2030 projects disrupted by security crisis.
SAU.global_detail: GCC's Article 51 invocation and joint air-defense activation represent a historic collective response. Gulf neutrality is effectively over.
SAU.bio: MBS faces the gravest external security crisis of his rule. The GCC invoked Article 51 of the UN Charter for collective self-defense. A second drone attack on Ras Tanura was intercepted. The UAE recalled its ambassador from Iran. Gulf states activated joint air-defense systems and reconnaissance flights. Analysts warn continued Iranian strikes could push Gulf states from defense into 'active theater of response.'

LBN.name: Naim Qassem
LBN.title: Secretary-General of Hezbollah
LBN.tagline: Hezbollah facing 250+ Israeli airstrikes and an imminent ground invasion as three IDF divisions mass on the border.
LBN.domestic_detail: Deep Shia loyalty but the decision to enter the war has drawn government and public condemnation. 800,000 people ordered to evacuate.
LBN.global_detail: Designated terrorist. Hezbollah's war entry triggered the largest Israeli military operation in Lebanon since 2006.
LBN.bio: Qassem's decision to enter the war has triggered devastating consequences. Israel has conducted 250+ airstrikes, killed Hezbollah intelligence chief Makled and other commanders, and is now preparing a ground invasion with three divisions. 800,000+ people ordered to evacuate. Hezbollah militants clashed with IDF forces near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra on March 4.

LBN.name: Nawaf Salam
LBN.title: Prime Minister of Lebanon
LBN.tagline: Demanding Hezbollah disarm as 800,000 Lebanese are ordered to evacuate ahead of Israeli ground invasion.
LBN.domestic_detail: His opposition to Hezbollah's war has broad support. The humanitarian crisis strengthens his position as the voice of Lebanese sovereignty.
LBN.global_detail: International community views Salam as the legitimate voice of Lebanon against Hezbollah's unilateral war decision.
LBN.bio: Salam continues demanding Hezbollah disarm as Israel prepares the largest ground operation in Lebanon since 2006. 800,000+ ordered to evacuate. The humanitarian catastrophe strengthens his case that Hezbollah's decision to enter the war was a disaster for Lebanon.

YEM.name: Abdul-Malik al-Houthi
YEM.title: Supreme Leader of Ansar Allah (the Houthis)
YEM.tagline: Creating an unprecedented dual chokepoint crisis — Red Sea attacks resume while Iran closes Hormuz.
YEM.domestic_detail: Commands genuine support in Houthi territory. Framing resumed attacks as defense of Iran and Islam.
YEM.global_detail: Designated Foreign Terrorist Organization. The dual chokepoint crisis (Hormuz + Red Sea) is the worst maritime disruption in decades.
YEM.bio: Al-Houthi's forces announced resumed Red Sea shipping attacks, ending a 3.5-month pause. Combined with Iran's Hormuz closure, this creates an unprecedented dual chokepoint crisis. Maersk already rerouting via Cape of Good Hope. UKMTO issued formal security advisories. Military analysts confirm the Houthis retained full capabilities during the pause.

RUS.name: Vladimir Putin
RUS.title: President of the Russian Federation
RUS.tagline: Using Iran crisis for anti-US propaganda while Ukraine damages Russian warships and strikes deep into Russia.
RUS.domestic_detail: State-controlled polling shows 70%+ approval. Iran crisis provides propaganda ammunition against the US.
RUS.global_detail: ICC warrant. Iran alliance exposed as hollow. Nuclear personnel depletion. Ukraine striking ever deeper into Russia.
RUS.bio: Putin's officials are using the Iran war to harshly criticize the US and position Russia alongside Iran as a victim of Western aggression. But material support remains zero. Meanwhile, Ukraine damaged a Russian frigate at a naval base and struck oil infrastructure 750+ miles from the border. Russian forces failed to advance on any axis on March 5. A new diplomatic dispute with Hungary over the Druzhba pipeline adds complications.

RUS.name: Andrei Belousov
RUS.title: Minister of Defense of the Russian Federation
RUS.tagline: Managing a war where Russia cannot advance on any axis while Ukraine strikes deeper into the homeland.
RUS.domestic_detail: Industrial output focus. Career officers bristle at economist leadership. Manpower crisis deepening.
RUS.global_detail: US, EU, UK, Canadian sanctions. Russia's inability to advance suggests operational exhaustion.
RUS.bio: Belousov faces a grim picture: Russian forces failed to advance on any axis on March 5. Ukraine is striking Russian naval bases and oil infrastructure hundreds of miles from the border. The ISW assessed Russia has 'decreased intensity of infiltration efforts' near Pokrovsk due to logistical constraints.

UKR.name: Volodymyr Zelensky
UKR.title: President of Ukraine
UKR.tagline: Ukraine striking Russian warships and oil infrastructure while downing 136 of 155 drones in one night.
UKR.domestic_detail: Trust polling around 61%. Battlefield successes provide morale but Hungary dispute adds diplomatic friction.
UKR.global_detail: European leaders and NATO view him as symbol of resistance. Gulf drone expertise sharing builds new alliances.
UKR.bio: Zelensky's forces damaged a Russian frigate, struck oil infrastructure 750+ miles from the border, and downed 136 of 155 drones in one night. Russian forces couldn't advance on any axis March 5. A new dispute with Hungary over the Druzhba pipeline and detained bank employees adds diplomatic complexity. Trilateral peace talks may move to Florida.

CHN.name: Xi Jinping
CHN.title: General Secretary of the CPC, President of China, Chairman of the CMC
CHN.tagline: Set GDP target at 4.5-5%, defense at $277B, launching Five-Year Plan focused on tech self-reliance amid global turmoil.
CHN.domestic_detail: Lower GDP target acknowledges headwinds. Youth unemployment and property crisis managed but not solved.
CHN.global_detail: Most sought-after partner for developing nations. Most feared by Western democracies. Xi-Trump summit March 31 looms.
CHN.bio: Xi oversaw the NPC setting a 4.5-5% GDP target (lowest in years), 7% defense spending increase to $277B, and launching the 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizing AI, chips, quantum tech, and energy security. R&D investment to grow 7%+ annually. The Iran war has elevated energy security as a top policy concern. Xi-Trump summit March 31-April 2 will be defining.

TWN.name: Lai Ching-te
TWN.title: President of Taiwan (Republic of China)
TWN.tagline: Facing China's $277B defense budget and delayed US arms while 2027 invasion warnings mount.
TWN.domestic_detail: Approval at 28-33%. Opposition blocks agenda. Impeachment proceedings ongoing.
TWN.global_detail: Viewed as sovereignty defender. Sharper rhetoric creates some allied anxiety.
TWN.bio: Lai faces China's confirmed 7% defense increase to $277B while the US arms package remains delayed for the Xi-Trump summit. Japan's 2027 invasion warning stands. TSMC chip dominance remains Taiwan's de facto security guarantee.

PRK.name: Kim Jong Un
PRK.title: General Secretary, Chairman of State Affairs Commission
PRK.tagline: Watched cruise missiles fire from new destroyer — expanding North Korea's naval strike capability while the world looks at Iran.
PRK.domestic_detail: Absolute authority. New destroyer demonstrates growing military capability to domestic audience.
PRK.global_detail: Under comprehensive UN sanctions. Russia alliance provides partial lifeline. New destroyer shows growing capability.
PRK.bio: Kim watched cruise missiles launch from the Choe Hyon, North Korea's newest destroyer, believed to carry dozens of potentially nuclear-capable missiles. This follows two ballistic missile tests in 2026. The Workers' Party five-year nuclear plan is in effect. Troops fight in Ukraine alongside Russia. The Iran crisis provides ideal cover.

SDN.name: Abdel Fattah al-Burhan
SDN.title: Commander of the Sudanese Armed Forces
SDN.tagline: Presiding over what the UN calls 'indicators of a genocidal path' as the war passes 1,000 days.
SDN.domestic_detail: Backed by military establishment. SAF drone strikes continue hitting civilian infrastructure.
SDN.global_detail: Backed by Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Turkey. Co-responsible for a conflict the UN fact-finding mission links to genocide indicators.
SDN.bio: Al-Burhan leads the SAF as the war passes 1,000 days. The UN Fact-Finding Mission described RSF atrocities as 'indicators of a genocidal path.' Up to 400,000 estimated killed. 12M+ displaced. Basic services near total collapse. El Obeid besieged. Chad lost 7 soldiers to RSF — the conflict is regionalizing.

SDN.name: Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo
SDN.title: Commander of the Rapid Support Forces (RSF)
SDN.tagline: Known as 'Hemedti' — his forces described by UN fact-finding mission as showing 'indicators of a genocidal path.'
SDN.domestic_detail: Commands loyalty in some communities. RSF widely accused of mass atrocities including those triggering genocide indicators.
SDN.global_detail: US Treasury sanctioned for genocide. ICC investigation. UAE support a major friction point. UN genocide indicators now documented.
SDN.bio: Hemedti's RSF was specifically named in the UN Fact-Finding Mission report for atrocities in El Fasher described as 'indicators of a genocidal path.' The RSF besieges El Obeid from three sides. Fighting across North Darfur, North Kordofan, South Kordofan, and Blue Nile. Chad border clashes show regionalization.

COD.name: Félix Tshisekedi
COD.title: President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo
COD.tagline: Accusing Rwanda of violating the Washington Accords as M23 consolidates gains.
COD.domestic_detail: Criticized for corruption but retains nationalist support against Rwanda.
COD.global_detail: Western capitals engage. US minerals-for-security deal continues.
COD.bio: Tshisekedi accuses Rwanda of violating the Washington Accords. M23 maintains control in eastern DRC. The minerals-for-security deal with the US remains his strongest leverage.

COD.name: Paul Kagame
COD.title: President of Rwanda
COD.tagline: Bankrolling M23 despite signing the Washington Accords.
COD.global_detail: Post-genocide transformation credited. M23 support condemned. ~4,000 RDF troops in eastern DRC.
COD.bio: Kagame signed the Washington Accords but Rwandan-backed forces continue operations. UN estimates 4,000 RDF troops inside eastern DRC.

VEN.name: Delcy Rodríguez
VEN.title: Acting President of Venezuela
VEN.tagline: 3,200+ released under amnesty but independent groups verify far fewer. Oil leverage growing as Hormuz closes.
VEN.domestic_detail: Accepted by Chavista base. Military and opposition skeptical.
VEN.global_detail: Most nations don't recognize legitimacy. Oil leverage growing as global supply tightens.
VEN.bio: Rodríguez oversaw 3,200+ releases under the amnesty law, but Foro Penal independently verifies only 91-109 political releases — a dramatic gap. Venezuela's oil reserves are the world's largest and increasingly strategic as Iran's Hormuz closure disrupts 20% of global oil.

HTI.name: Alix Didier Fils-Aimé
HTI.title: Prime Minister and Head of Government of Haiti
HTI.tagline: Opened electoral inscriptions as a step toward elections despite 90% gang control of the capital.
HTI.domestic_detail: Backed by private sector and diaspora. Grassroots skeptical.
HTI.global_detail: US, UN, international institutions back him as best path to stability.
HTI.bio: Fils-Aimé's government opened electoral inscriptions March 2-12. Elections planned late August. Police chief says security plan in development. 900 new cadets graduated but force drastically understaffed.

HTI.name: Jimmy Chérizier
HTI.title: Head of Viv Ansanm Gang Coalition ("Barbecue")
HTI.tagline: Controls 90% of Port-au-Prince as the government tries to plan elections.
HTI.domestic_detail: Robin Hood image in some areas. Associated with massacres, rape, displacement elsewhere.
HTI.global_detail: US indicted with $5M reward. Viv Ansanm designated FTO.
HTI.bio: Chérizier's coalition controls 90% of Port-au-Prince and expanding into central Haiti. 1.4M displaced. 5,900+ killed in 2025. Children recruited from age 10.
